title: Market Brief — Monday, March 30, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 20% (56/280), H200 at 24% (90/368), and B200 is loosest at 32% (106/330) but still tight. The biggest move: H100 spot crashed 11.9% overnight to $1.47/hr while H200 climbed 8.3% to $2.52, signaling demand rotating from legacy silicon into next-gen capacity.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 20% (56/280), H200 at 24% (90/368), and B200 is loosest at 32% (106/330) but still tight. The biggest move: H100 spot crashed 11.9% overnight to $1.47/hr while H200 climbed 8.3% to $2.52, signaling demand rotating from legacy silicon into next-gen capacity.

At TCO-adjusted costs (1.25x spot), Llama 70B inference runs $5.36/M on H100, $6.03/M on H200, and $6.84/M on B200. Medium-tier API providers are selling at massive losses: negative 82% margin on B200 means burning roughly $5.60/M tokens below true cost. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is priced far below any self-hosted option, either subsidized or operating at extraordinary scale efficiency. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued when GPT-5.4 benchmarks comparably at $15/M, an 80% discount for near-equivalent reasoning. DeepSeek and Meta's open-weight models at sub-$0.60/M output are the undervalued trade: buy inference capacity on loosening B200 supply at $3.00/hr floor and arbitrage against premium API pricing.
bullets: 20% H100 availability (56 of 280 GPUs) qualifies as severe shortage territory approaching the 15% threshold.
32% B200 availability (106 of 330 GPUs) makes it the loosest compute class but still below comfortable supply levels.
$1.47/hr H100 spot dropped 11.9% day-over-day, the largest single-day GPU price decline in the dataset.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 undercuts the next cheapest model by 24%.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means token pricing has fallen roughly 79% from the GPT-4 launch baseline of 100.
$80/M output for o3 Pro is 211x more expensive than DeepSeek V3.2 for frontier reasoning access.
